title: NBA Daily Digest
subtitle: July 2, 2026 - Offseason Blockbusters & Free Agency Frenzy!

items.headline: Blockbuster! Celtics Trade Jaylen Brown to 76ers for Paul George, Picks
items.summary: In a seismic shift in the Eastern Conference, the Boston Celtics have traded 2024 NBA Finals MVP and five-time All-Star Jaylen Brown to the Philadelphia 76ers. The Celtics receive Paul George, two first-round picks, and two second-round picks in return. Brown is coming off a stellar season, averaging 28.7 points, 6.9 rebounds, and 5.1 assists per game.

items.headline: Lakers Make Waves: Kessler Acquired, LeBron Departs, Multiple Free Agent Signings
items.summary: The Los Angeles Lakers have been exceptionally active, acquiring big man Walker Kessler from the Utah Jazz for a package including unprotected first-round picks in 2031 and 2033, and first-round swaps in 2028 and 2030. Kessler is set to sign a four-year, $130 million deal. This comes as LeBron James is reportedly departing the Lakers as a free agent after eight seasons, prioritizing happiness over money in his next move. The Lakers also bolstered their roster with free agent signings, bringing in Sandro Mamukelashvili (four-year, $52M), Collin Sexton (two-year, $19M), and Quentin Grimes (four-year, $60M).

items.headline: No NBA Games Reported
items.summary: No regular NBA games were played on July 1, 2026, as the league is currently in its offseason with teams focused on trades and free agency.

items.headline: Deni Avdija: All-Star Season & Untouchable Asset
items.summary: Portland Trail Blazers All-Star forward Deni Avdija continues to be a central figure for his team, with reports indicating he is considered 'untouchable' in trade discussions. Last season, Avdija posted impressive averages of 24.2 points, 6.9 rebounds, and 6.7 assists over 425 games. He also recorded a notable 30-point performance on April 19, 2026, against the San Antonio Spurs.

items.headline: Jaylen Brown's Dominant Season Before Trade
items.summary: Before his blockbuster trade to Philadelphia, Jaylen Brown delivered an outstanding 2025-26 season for the Boston Celtics, averaging 28.7 points, 6.9 rebounds, and 5.1 assists per game. His exceptional play earned him NBA Finals MVP honors in 2024 and five All-Star selections.

items.headline: Israeli-American Rookie Emanuel Sharp Joins Kings
items.summary: Israeli-American guard Emanuel Sharp has signed a three-year, $6.35 million contract with the Sacramento Kings. Sharp, a recent draft pick, is projected as a '3-and-D' specialist known for his high basketball IQ, solid three-point shot, and strong defensive effort. He joins Deni Avdija, Ben Saraf, and Danny Wolf as the fourth Israeli player currently in the NBA.

items.headline: Sandro Mamukelashvili's Career Year
items.summary: Forward Sandro Mamukelashvili, a new Lakers signing, is coming off a career-best year with the Toronto Raptors. He averaged 11.2 points, shot 52.3% from the field, and recorded 0.8 steals and 0.5 blocks per game in the 2025-26 season.

items.headline: Offseason Shakes Up League with Multiple Star Moves
items.summary: Beyond the Jaylen Brown and LeBron James news, the NBA offseason has already seen several other blockbuster moves reshaping the league. Notable transactions include Giannis Antetokounmpo heading from Milwaukee to Miami, Kawhi Leonard and Brandon Ingram swapping teams between Toronto and the Los Angeles Clippers, and Ja Morant being traded from Memphis to Portland.

items.headline: Celtics Bolster Defense with Mitchell Robinson Signing
items.summary: The Boston Celtics have signed current NBA champion Mitchell Robinson as a free agent from the New York Knicks. The elite rim protector agreed to a three-year, $47.4 million deal, expected to significantly upgrade Boston's defense. The Celtics also added Mike Conley on a one-year contract.

items.headline: All-Star Norman Powell Heads to Chicago Bulls
items.summary: Norman Powell, an All-Star last season, is set to join the Chicago Bulls after agreeing to a two-year deal that could be worth up to $45 million. This move marks another significant player changing teams in a busy offseason.

items.headline: Nikola Vucevic Makes Orlando Magic Return
items.summary: After five years, Nikola Vucevic has returned to the Orlando Magic, signing a one-year contract worth $3.9 million. Vucevic, one of the franchise's all-time leading players, reportedly turned down more lucrative offers to rejoin the team where he spent the best years of his career.

items.headline: 2026 NBA Cup Schedule Announced for Pacers
items.summary: The Indiana Pacers have a portion of their 2026 NBA Cup schedule set. They will host NBA Cup games against the defending champion New York Knicks and the Philadelphia 76ers at Gainbridge Fieldhouse. Additionally, the Pacers will play road games against the Cleveland Cavaliers and the Miami Heat.
